# WhiteBit.Net

> Strongly typed .NET client library for the WhiteBit REST and WebSocket APIs. Supports V4 public market data, spot trading, collateral/futures trading, balances, deposits, withdrawals, convert, WhiteBit Codes, sub-accounts, private WebSocket streams, local order books, and CryptoExchange.Net shared APIs.

WhiteBit.Net is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem. It uses `WebCallResult<T>` / `CallResult<T>` for error-aware results, supports client-side rate limiting, automatic WebSocket reconnection, dependency injection, native AOT, and shared API abstractions for multi-exchange applications. Current repository package version: 3.11.1. Targets netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.

## Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/README.md): Overview, installation, examples, supported functionality, release notes
- [Documentation Site](https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/WhiteBit.Net/): Full documentation hub and client reference
- [AI API Quick Map](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/docs/ai-api-map.md): Table-style intent-to-method routing for WhiteBit.Net members
- [Full LLM Context](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/llms-full.txt): Detailed AI guide with code patterns, endpoint routing, and anti-hallucination rules
- [Claude/Codex Skill](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/CLAUDE.md): Assistant-facing usage conventions and examples

## Examples

- [AI-friendly examples directory](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/tree/main/Examples/ai-friendly): Compilable single-file examples optimized for AI assistants
- [Spot Quickstart](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/01-spot-quickstart.cs): Client setup, ticker lookup, balances, spot order, cancel order
- [Collateral/Futures Trading](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/02-collateral-futures.cs): Leverage, collateral order, positions, reduce-only close
- [WebSocket Streams](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs): Public ticker/kline streams and private order/balance streams with teardown
- [Multi-Exchange Pattern](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/04-multi-exchange.cs): CryptoExchange.Net SharedApis usage
- [Error Handling](https://github.com/JKorf/WhiteBit.Net/blob/main/Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs): `WebCallResult` checks, transient retry, validation handling

## Critical Rules

- Use `WhiteBitRestClient` and `WhiteBitSocketClient`; do not generate raw HTTP.
- Use `WhiteBitCredentials("key", "secret")`; do not use generic `ApiCredentials`.
- Direct client calls use `V4Api`, not `SpotApi`, `FuturesApi`, or `GeneralApi`.
- Direct spot symbols use WhiteBit format such as `ETH_USDT`; collateral/futures examples use `ETH_PERP`.
- Always check `.Success` before `.Data`.
- For spot orders use `restClient.V4Api.Trading.PlaceSpotOrderAsync`.
- For collateral/futures orders use `restClient.V4Api.CollateralTrading.PlaceOrderAsync`.
- For multi-exchange code use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` from `.V4Api.SharedClient`.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and call `socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(subscription.Data)`.

## Reference

- [CryptoExchange.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoExchange.Net): Base library
- [CryptoClients.Net](https://github.com/JKorf/CryptoClients.Net): Bundle package for all supported exchange clients
- [NuGet Package](https://www.nuget.org/packages/WhiteBit.Net): Stable package
- [Discord Community](https://discord.gg/MSpeEtSY8t): Maintainer-supported Discord
